message:
fix(storage): reopen connection after bulk import to clear stale fsqlite snapshot state

src/storage/sqlite.rs::SqliteStorage
  Add `reopen_at(path, lock_timeout_ms)` which drops `self` and
  replaces it with a freshly opened connection at the same path
  (via `Self::open_with_timeout`). The doc-comment spells out the
  load-bearing reason: fsqlite's pager / MVCC state is per-
  connection, and after a large bulk import the in-memory view
  can lag behind what was actually flushed to disk. Subsequent
  VACUUM, REINDEX, and even fresh OpenRead cursors then fail
  with stale-snapshot errors:
    "database is busy (snapshot conflict on pages: page N >
     snapshot db_size M)"
    "OpenRead failed: could not open storage cursor on root
     page N"
  Dropping the connection forces fsqlite to re-read its view on
  the next open, which makes the post-rebuild cleanup actually
  work. The drop-then-open ordering is required because fsqlite
  tracks pages by file path and two live connections to the same
  DB can themselves surface BusySnapshot conflicts.

src/config/mod.rs::rebuild_database_family
  Call `storage.reopen_at(db_path, lock_timeout)` after
  `import_from_jsonl` and before the VACUUM / REINDEX cleanup
  block. Without this the cleanup ran against the stale snapshot
  and silently failed (was logged at `tracing::debug!` so the
  failure was effectively invisible). Also bump both VACUUM and
  REINDEX failure logs from `debug!` → `warn!` and add the
  `db_path` field, so a real failure (now rare) is actually
  surfaced.

src/output/context.rs::OutputContext::section
  Re-enable the previously commented-out `.style(self.theme()
  .section.clone())` on the section-rule renderer. Same shape as
  the `error` panel re-enable from the previous commit — the
  previous gating through a `theme()` accessor left the call
  commented out as `// .style(self.theme.section.clone())`. The
  tone now renders correctly.

Cargo.lock left modified intentionally — same local-sibling-path
patch override situation flagged in the previous beads_rust
passes.
